outlook.headline: Demand is improving, but breadth and mentions remain soft
outlook.summary: Automotive MCU demand looks firmer in select names, led by Renesas’ rising wafer inputs and newer-generation ramp, plus solid linked demand at Diodes and healthy customer interest around Synaptics. But the story is not yet broad-based: Microchip called MCUs merely fine, and overall mentions are still down 8% vs the trailing average, suggesting the rebound is still selective rather than sector-wide.

drivers.label: Renesas demand and ramp-up
drivers.detail: Management said MCU demand is increasing, wafer input is being raised, and newer generations are ramping well.

drivers.label: New design wins and deployment activity
drivers.detail: CEVA cited a new MCU-partner win and first mass-volume automotive AI deployment, signaling active customer adoption.

drivers.label: Healthy MCU-adjacent demand
drivers.detail: Diodes said linked MCU power-supply and motor-drive applications are seeing solid to significant demand.

drivers.label: Pipeline still healthy at Synaptics
drivers.detail: New product launches, awards, and upcoming sampling point to sustained customer interest and near-term follow-through.

drivers.label: Broad-based strength not yet visible
drivers.detail: Microchip described microcontrollers as fine but did not call out special strength, and mention momentum is still below trend.

risks: Mention momentum remains 8% below the trailing average, which could signal slower sector-wide re-acceleration.
If Microchip-style 'fine' demand proves more representative, the current improvement may stay narrow.
Watch whether Renesas wafer input increases translate into sustained orders or just inventory normalization.
